comment_text: Kevin Durant's steal against Serbia was clearly a foul, and there was also a backcourt violation that was not called. The US team should have easily defeated their adversaries, but they didn't, and to beat them, everything needs to be perfect, including the referees. This situation was reminiscent of the officials manipulation during the Mavericks-Miami series, shaq and wade miami. The referees kept the USA in the game to allow Steph Curry to shine. Many people may have forgotten this, but I haven't.

comment_text: I knew whenI saw more cameras in Basketball Olympics that US basketball team was up to some shady stuff. I can say that when Serbia lost to US not many people paid attention to US taking the gold (social media activity post and view count was down). People complained with the overwhelming commercialization with the US team promotion to watch their final and blocking other European teams from promoting themselves. I am disappointed Netflix didn't do the behind the scenes with South Sudan because they were amazing and should get the recognized. I think that months later the US basketball team is trying to gain some monetary value with this docu series because all focus came on Serbia during the metal ceremony. If there is clear favourtism and gaslighting during this series towards all other European teams then you will know whats up.

video_title: Court of Gold | Official Trailer | Netflix
comment_text:
I know this is an extremely long comment but I just wanna share my excitement and analysis on this anticipated documentary:

Finally, after 2 separate Netflix documentary films featuring the 1992 Dream Team and the 2008 Redeem Team, the Team USA Men's 5x5 Basketball has another doc featuring "The Avengers" on Paris 2024 Summer Olympics but this time it features the men's national 5x5 basketball teams of Serbia, France, and Canada because we got used to hearing from the perspectives of the USABMNT only. 

I actually like this kind of POV because it shows that even before USABMNT (United States of America Basketball Men's National Team) won the 5th consecutive and 17th overall Olympic gold medal for men's 5x5 basketball (which just further elevated, expanded, and increased their status and reputation of being the dominant team, the powerhouses, and the legends of international basketball), basketball has increasingly become a global market for international athletes which has now dispelled the myth that basketball only exists in the U.S. (well, that's wrong anyway) and that the USABMNT are unbeatable and unstoppable.

Well, the thing is, the last 2 games (semifinals vs Serbia then the final vs France) at Paris 2024 really brought the high stakes pressure, intensity, and challenge test for USABMNT because they always aim to win for gold yet somehow after Beijing 2008 their unbeatable status in every match has changed to vulnerable at times and the world is catching up to their level gradually. But every time they showed up, they delivered. Steph Curry, Kevin Durant, and LeBron James really brought their A-game on this one teaming up together. They deserved the gold for sure!

Now, here's a catch. I'm extremely excited for Los Angeles 2028; for sure, Team USA in all sports will certainly collect so many gold medals more than any other country and earn the top spot at the overall medal table for the 20th time. USABMNT will 100% certainly win their 6th consecutive and 18th overall Olympic gold medal, but likely the competition will be tougher and tougher from their opponents. They may maintain that perfect win-loss record from the exhibition games down to the final match, but after LA28 I'm realistic to note that it won't be the same as 1984, 1992, 2008 or 2012.
